words.example: Blade bolt tensioning is part of the routine maintenance checklist.
words.definition_en: The process of applying controlled torque to bolts connecting wind turbine blade sections to ensure structural integrity.
words.example_real: Site team reported unusual noise from the blade bolt tensioning — I want engineering to do a root cause analysis.

words.example: The blade bearing allows each blade to rotate for optimal pitch angle.
words.definition_en: A large-diameter slewing bearing that connects the blade root to the hub and enables pitch adjustment.
words.example_real: The blade bearing reading on turbine #7 was out of spec, so we're sending a crew up first thing tomorrow.

words.example: Pitch motors control the angle of attack for each blade independently.
words.definition_en: An electric motor that drives the pitch system to rotate each blade around its longitudinal axis.
words.example_real: We've extended the pitch motor inspection interval by six months based on good condition history.

words.example: Pitch bearings must withstand high bending moments during operation.
words.definition_en: A specialized slewing bearing mounted between the blade root and hub to facilitate pitch rotation under load.
words.example_real: We'll need to swap out the pitch bearing during the next planned outage — let's get the spare ordered now.

words.example: Hydraulic pitch cylinders provide rapid blade angle adjustments.
words.definition_en: A hydraulic actuator that converts fluid pressure into linear motion to control blade pitch angle.
words.example_real: Can you double-check the pitch cylinder status on the north string? SCADA flagged an intermittent fault overnight.

words.example: The hub casting undergoes rigorous non-destructive testing before assembly.
words.definition_en: A large cast iron component forming the central connection point for all three blades on a horizontal-axis turbine.
words.example_real: After that windstorm, we did a walkdown and found three hub casting issues. Nothing critical, but worth scheduling.

words.example: The spinner covers the hub for aerodynamic and aesthetic purposes.
words.definition_en: The aerodynamic nose cone covering the rotor hub to reduce drag and protect internal components from weather.
words.example_real: I need your numbers on spinner before Friday. I'm presenting to the client's technical team.

words.example: The bedplate supports the entire drive train and generator assembly.
words.definition_en: A heavy cast or welded steel frame inside the nacelle that provides the structural foundation for the gearbox and generator.
words.example_real: We just finished the bedplate design review. Some good ideas came up for cutting cost without sacrificing quality.

words.example: The yaw ring is a critical component for nacelle orientation.
words.definition_en: A large-diameter gear ring mounted between the nacelle and tower top, engaged by yaw motors to rotate the nacelle.
words.example_real: Make sure the crew reviews the yaw ring JSA before starting work — it's a high-risk activity.

words.example: Yaw brakes hold the nacelle in position after alignment.
words.definition_en: Hydraulic or spring-applied brake calipers that lock the nacelle at the correct yaw angle once aligned with wind direction.
words.example_real: The yaw brake failed on turbine #12 during the grid dip — we're mobilizing the spare and a technician.

words.example: The yaw drive rotates the nacelle to align with wind direction.
words.definition_en: A motorized gearbox assembly that engages the yaw ring to rotate the nacelle in response to wind direction changes.
words.example_real: The yaw drive failed on turbine #12 during the grid dip — we're mobilizing the spare and a technician.

words.example: The slip ring transfers electrical power and signals between rotating and stationary parts.
words.definition_en: An electromechanical device allowing the transmission of power and data from the rotating hub to the stationary nacelle.
words.example_real: We'll need to swap out the slip ring during the next planned outage — let's get the spare ordered now.

words.example: Carbon brushes in the generator require periodic replacement.
words.definition_en: A sliding electrical contact made of carbon-graphite composite that conducts current between stationary and rotating parts.
words.example_real: In the weekly coordination call, we agreed carbon brush is the priority — if it slips, COD moves.

words.example: Generator bearing failure is a common cause of turbine downtime.
words.definition_en: Rolling-element bearings supporting the generator rotor shaft, typically requiring condition monitoring for vibration and temperature.
words.example_real: We've extended the generator bearing inspection interval by six months based on good condition history.

words.example: The main shaft bearing supports the entire rotor assembly.
words.definition_en: A large spherical roller or tapered roller bearing that carries the rotor's weight and thrust loads at the low-speed shaft end.
words.example_real: I was on site yesterday — the main shaft bearing looks fine, but I'd like us to trend the data for a few more weeks.

words.example: Always engage the rotor lock before performing maintenance inside the hub.
words.definition_en: A mechanical pin or disc brake that physically prevents rotor rotation during maintenance for personnel safety.
words.example_real: After that windstorm, we did a walkdown and found three rotor lock issues. Nothing critical, but worth scheduling.

words.example: The brake disc must be inspected for warping and cracks.
words.definition_en: A metal disc mounted on the high-speed shaft that works with calipers to stop or hold the rotor.
words.example_real: Site team reported unusual noise from the brake disc — I want engineering to do a root cause analysis.

words.example: Brake calipers apply clamping force to the brake disc.
words.definition_en: A hydraulic or spring-actuated clamping device that presses brake pads against the disc to generate stopping torque.
words.example_real: The brake caliper reading on turbine #7 was out of spec, so we're sending a crew up first thing tomorrow.

words.example: The hydraulic power unit supplies pressure for pitch and brake systems.
words.definition_en: An integrated assembly of pump, motor, reservoir, valves and accumulator providing pressurized fluid to turbine hydraulic systems.
words.example_real: The power unit PM is due next Tuesday. I've blocked the turbine from 6 AM to 2 PM.

words.example: The accumulator stores hydraulic energy for emergency pitch actuation.
words.definition_en: A pressure vessel that stores hydraulic fluid under gas pressure to provide emergency power for blade pitching during grid loss.
words.example_real: The accumulator geotech results came in — we'll need to adjust the foundation design in that area.

words.example: The cooling fan maintains optimal temperature inside the nacelle.
words.definition_en: An electric fan that circulates air through the nacelle or heat exchangers to dissipate heat from the generator and gearbox.
words.example_real: We spotted an issue with the cooling fan during this morning's walkdown — logged and assigned to the day shift.

words.example: The oil cooler prevents the gearbox from overheating.
words.definition_en: A heat exchanger that removes excess heat from lubricating oil using air or water as the cooling medium.
words.example_real: The oil cooler data over the past quarter shows a clear pattern — worth discussing with the OEM.

words.example: The nacelle crane lifts tools and spare parts during maintenance.
words.definition_en: A small overhead or jib crane installed inside the nacelle for handling components during repair and maintenance activities.
words.example_real: Can you double-check the nacelle crane status on the north string? SCADA flagged an intermittent fault overnight.

words.example: A service lift inside the tower reduces technician fatigue.
words.definition_en: A climb-assist elevator installed inside the turbine tower to transport personnel and tools to the nacelle level.
words.example_real: Your service lift proposal looks solid. A couple of points on the warranty — let's discuss.

words.example: Climb assist systems reduce physical strain on technicians.
words.definition_en: A motorized or counterweight system that helps technicians ascend the tower ladder with reduced effort.
words.example_real: The climb assist work package is at 60% — tracking slightly behind, but recovery plan is in place.

words.example: Every technician must use a ladder fall arrester inside the tower.
words.definition_en: A safety device that runs along a rail and locks instantly if a climber falls, preventing injury during tower ascent.
words.example_real: Make sure the crew reviews the ladder fall arrester JSA before starting work — it's a high-risk activity.

words.example: Tower flanges connect adjacent tower sections with high-strength bolts.
words.definition_en: A forged steel ring welded to each tower section end, providing a bolted connection interface between sections.
words.example_real: Can you double-check the tower flange status on the north string? SCADA flagged an intermittent fault overnight.

words.example: The tower door provides access to the turbine interior.
words.definition_en: A weather-sealed access door at the base of the wind turbine tower, typically equipped with safety interlocks.
words.example_real: The tower door reading on turbine #7 was out of spec, so we're sending a crew up first thing tomorrow.

words.example: The anchor cage is embedded in the concrete foundation.
words.definition_en: A steel reinforcement cage with anchor bolts cast into the concrete foundation to secure the tower base.
words.example_real: We spotted an issue with the anchor cage during this morning's walkdown — logged and assigned to the day shift.

words.example: High-strength grout fills the gap between tower and foundation.
words.definition_en: A cementitious material pumped into the annular space between the tower base and foundation to ensure uniform load transfer.
words.example_real: After that windstorm, we did a walkdown and found three grout issues. Nothing critical, but worth scheduling.

words.example: Use a calibrated bolt tensioner for flange connections.
words.definition_en: A hydraulic tool that applies precise axial tension to bolts for achieving specified preload in structural connections.
words.example_real: We'll need to swap out the bolt tensioner during the next planned outage — let's get the spare ordered now.

words.example: A torque wrench ensures bolts are tightened to specification.
words.definition_en: A precision hand tool that applies a specific rotational force to fasteners and indicates when the target torque is reached.
words.example_real: We're seeing some unusual readings on the torque wrench — let's pull the trend data for the last 30 days.

words.example: The blade root connects to the hub via T-bolts or studs.
words.definition_en: The thickest structural section of a wind turbine blade where it attaches to the pitch bearing and hub.
words.example_real: The blade root PM is due next Tuesday. I've blocked the turbine from 6 AM to 2 PM.

words.example: Blade tips can reach speeds over 300 km/h at rated power.
words.definition_en: The outermost section of a wind turbine blade that experiences the highest linear velocity and contributes most to aerodynamic torque.
words.example_real: Site team reported unusual noise from the blade tip — I want engineering to do a root cause analysis.

words.example: The blade spar provides the primary structural strength.
words.definition_en: The main longitudinal load-carrying structural element inside a blade, typically made of carbon fiber or glass fiber reinforced composites.
words.example_real: The blade spar failed on turbine #12 during the grid dip — we're mobilizing the spare and a technician.

words.example: The blade shell forms the aerodynamic profile.
words.definition_en: The outer aerodynamic surface of a wind turbine blade, typically a composite sandwich structure of glass fiber and core material.
words.example_real: I was on site yesterday — the blade shell looks fine, but I'd like us to trend the data for a few more weeks.

words.example: Lightning receptors channel strikes safely to ground.
words.definition_en: Metallic points embedded along the blade surface that capture lightning current and direct it through an internal conductor to the grounding system.
words.example_real: Make sure the crew reviews the lightning receptor JSA before starting work — it's a high-risk activity.

words.example: The down conductor carries lightning current from blade to ground.
words.definition_en: An internal cable or conductive path that routes lightning current from receptors through the blade and tower to the earthing system.
words.example_real: RFQ responses for down conductor are in. Three suppliers shortlisted — site visits planned for next month.

words.example: Vortex generators improve blade aerodynamic performance.
words.definition_en: Small aerodynamic fins attached to the blade surface that generate micro-vortices to delay flow separation and increase lift.
words.example_real: We've extended the vortex generator inspection interval by six months based on good condition history.

words.example: Serrated trailing edges reduce aerodynamic noise.
words.definition_en: A sawtooth-profile attachment on the blade trailing edge designed to break up turbulent flow and reduce noise emissions.
words.example_real: Can you send over the updated serrated trailing edge datasheet? Our engineering team has a few technical queries.

words.example: Leading edge erosion reduces blade efficiency significantly.
words.definition_en: The progressive material loss from the blade leading edge caused by rain, hail, sand, and UV exposure over time.
words.example_real: Site team reported unusual noise from the leading edge erosion — I want engineering to do a root cause analysis.

words.example: Leading edge protection extends blade service life.
words.definition_en: Applied tape, coating, or shield systems that protect the blade leading edge from rain and particle erosion.
words.example_real: We need to verify the leading edge protection protection settings before energization. No shortcuts on safety.

words.example: Blade repair requires specialized composite material skills.
words.definition_en: The process of restoring blade structural integrity and aerodynamic profile through composite lamination and fairing techniques.
words.example_real: Make sure the crew reviews the blade repair JSA before starting work — it's a high-risk activity.

words.example: Regular blade inspection prevents catastrophic failures.
words.definition_en: Systematic examination of blade surfaces and internal structures using visual, drone, or non-destructive testing methods.
words.example_real: The blade inspection reading on turbine #7 was out of spec, so we're sending a crew up first thing tomorrow.

words.example: Structural health monitoring provides early warning of issues.
words.definition_en: Continuous or periodic monitoring system using sensors to assess the condition of critical structural components in real time.
words.example_real: Can you double-check the health monitoring status on the north string? SCADA flagged an intermittent fault overnight.

words.example: Blade balancing minimizes vibration during operation.
words.definition_en: The process of ensuring the three rotor blades have equal mass moments to prevent excessive vibration and bearing loads.
words.example_real: We've extended the blade balancing inspection interval by six months based on good condition history.

words.example: Rotor balancing is essential for smooth turbine operation.
words.definition_en: Adjusting mass distribution of the entire rotor assembly to minimize vibration forces transmitted to the drive train.
words.example_real: After that windstorm, we did a walkdown and found three rotor balancing issues. Nothing critical, but worth scheduling.

words.example: Scheduled maintenance is performed at fixed intervals.
words.definition_en: Pre-planned maintenance activities carried out according to a time or production-based schedule to ensure continued safe operation.
words.example_real: I was on site yesterday — the scheduled maintenance looks fine, but I'd like us to trend the data for a few more weeks.

words.example: Condition-based maintenance reduces unnecessary service visits.
words.definition_en: A maintenance strategy where interventions are triggered by the actual condition of equipment rather than fixed time intervals.
words.example_real: The condition-based maintenance failed on turbine #12 during the grid dip — we're mobilizing the spare and a technician.

words.example: Predictive maintenance uses data analytics to forecast failures.
words.definition_en: Advanced maintenance approach using real-time sensor data and AI algorithms to predict equipment failure before it occurs.
words.example_real: We spotted an issue with the predictive maintenance during this morning's walkdown — logged and assigned to the day shift.

words.example: Reactive maintenance addresses failures after they occur.
words.definition_en: Repair or replacement performed only after equipment has failed, also known as run-to-failure maintenance.
words.example_real: The reactive maintenance PM is due next Tuesday. I've blocked the turbine from 6 AM to 2 PM.

words.example: Annual service covers comprehensive inspection and maintenance.
words.definition_en: A thorough yearly maintenance event that includes all scheduled checks, oil changes, filter replacements, and functional tests.
words.example_real: The EPC contractor raised a flag on annual service — I've put it on the risk register with a mitigation plan.

words.example: Service campaigns cover multiple turbines efficiently.
words.definition_en: A coordinated maintenance operation targeting a specific task or inspection across many turbines in a defined time window.
words.example_real: Thanks for the service campaign quote. We'll run it through the commercial review and get back to you.

words.example: End of warranty inspections are critical before handover.
words.definition_en: The period when the manufacturer's warranty expires, typically triggering a comprehensive inspection before responsibility transfers to the owner.
words.example_real: The end of warranty geotech results came in — we'll need to adjust the foundation design in that area.

words.example: Submit warranty claims promptly for defective components.
words.definition_en: A formal request to the manufacturer or supplier for repair or replacement of a component under the terms of the warranty agreement.
words.example_real: We just finished the warranty claim design review. Some good ideas came up for cutting cost without sacrificing quality.

words.example: Understanding failure modes improves maintenance planning.
words.definition_en: The specific way in which a component or system fails, described by its observable characteristics and root cause mechanism.
words.example_real: We'll need to swap out the failure mode during the next planned outage — let's get the spare ordered now.

words.example: Root cause analysis prevents recurring failures.
words.definition_en: A systematic problem-solving method used to identify the fundamental cause of a failure or problem, not just its symptoms.
words.example_real: The cause analysis data over the past quarter shows a clear pattern — worth discussing with the OEM.

words.example: Improving MTBF is a key reliability goal.
words.definition_en: A reliability metric representing the average operational time between one failure and the next for a repairable system.
words.example_real: The mean time between failure commissioning went really smoothly — your site engineer was excellent.

words.example: Reducing MTTR minimizes production losses.
words.definition_en: The average time required to diagnose, access, repair, and return a failed component to operational status.
words.example_real: The to repair PM is due next Tuesday. I've blocked the turbine from 6 AM to 2 PM.

words.example: The availability guarantee specifies minimum uptime percentage.
words.definition_en: A contractual commitment from the turbine supplier that the wind farm will achieve a specified percentage of time-based or energy-based availability.
words.example_real: Thanks for the availability guarantee quote. We'll run it through the commercial review and get back to you.

words.example: KPIs track operational performance of the wind farm.
words.definition_en: Quantifiable metrics used to evaluate the success of wind farm operations against defined targets and benchmarks.
words.example_real: I need your numbers on key performance indicator before Friday. I'm presenting to the client's technical team.

words.example: Each maintenance task is tracked through a work order.
words.definition_en: A formal document authorizing and tracking a specific maintenance, repair, or inspection task from creation to completion.
words.example_real: In the weekly coordination call, we agreed work order is the priority — if it slips, COD moves.

words.example: Effective spare parts management prevents extended downtime.
words.definition_en: The systematic planning, procurement, storage, and distribution of replacement components to support maintenance operations.
words.example_real: The parts management work package is at 60% — tracking slightly behind, but recovery plan is in place.

words.example: Critical spares must be available on site or nearby.
words.definition_en: Spare parts identified as essential for maintaining turbine availability due to long lead times or high failure impact.
words.example_real: RFQ responses for critical spares are in. Three suppliers shortlisted — site visits planned for next month.

words.example: Optimizing inventory turnover balances cost and availability.
words.definition_en: A ratio measuring how many times spare parts inventory is consumed and replenished over a given period.
words.example_real: Make sure the crew reviews the inventory turnover JSA before starting work — it's a high-risk activity.

words.example: The maintenance reserve fund covers major component replacement.
words.definition_en: A financial provision set aside over the project life to fund future major maintenance events and component replacements.
words.example_real: The maintenance reserve reading on turbine #7 was out of spec, so we're sending a crew up first thing tomorrow.

words.example: Regular oil sampling detects early signs of wear.
words.definition_en: The process of collecting lubricating oil specimens for laboratory analysis to assess contamination, wear metals, and fluid condition.
words.example_real: Can you double-check the oil sampling status on the north string? SCADA flagged an intermittent fault overnight.

words.example: Vibration analysis detects early bearing and gear defects.
words.definition_en: A condition monitoring technique using accelerometers to measure mechanical vibration and identify developing faults in rotating equipment.
words.example_real: The vibration analysis failed on turbine #12 during the grid dip — we're mobilizing the spare and a technician.

words.example: Oil debris monitoring provides real-time wear detection.
words.definition_en: Online sensors that continuously count and classify metallic particles in lubricating oil to detect abnormal wear rates.
words.example_real: We're seeing some unusual readings on the debris monitoring — let's pull the trend data for the last 30 days.

words.example: Endoscopic inspection examines gearbox internals without disassembly.
words.definition_en: Visual inspection of internal components using a flexible borescope inserted through access ports, minimizing disassembly requirements.
words.example_real: We'll need to swap out the endoscopic inspection during the next planned outage — let's get the spare ordered now.

words.example: Ultrasonic testing detects subsurface flaws in bolts and welds.
words.definition_en: A non-destructive testing method using high-frequency sound waves to detect internal defects in materials and measure thickness.
words.example_real: We spotted an issue with the ultrasonic testing during this morning's walkdown — logged and assigned to the day shift.

words.example: Magnetic particle inspection reveals surface cracks in steel.
words.definition_en: An NDT method where magnetic particles are applied to detect surface and near-surface discontinuities in ferromagnetic materials.
words.example_real: After that windstorm, we did a walkdown and found three magnetic particle inspection issues. Nothing critical, but worth scheduling.

words.example: Drone inspection provides fast and safe blade surveys.
words.definition_en: The use of unmanned aerial vehicles equipped with high-resolution cameras to inspect turbine blades and towers from the air.
words.example_real: We've extended the drone inspection inspection interval by six months based on good condition history.

words.example: Rope access techniques enable blade repairs without cranes.
words.definition_en: A method of accessing difficult-to-reach locations on wind turbines using industrial climbing and rope work techniques.
words.example_real: The EPC contractor raised a flag on rope access — I've put it on the risk register with a mitigation plan.

words.example: Tower inspection checks for structural integrity and corrosion.
words.definition_en: Visual and instrumental examination of the tower interior and exterior for corrosion, cracking, bolt loosening, and coating degradation.
words.example_real: Site team reported unusual noise from the tower inspection — I want engineering to do a root cause analysis.

words.example: Foundation inspection checks for settlement and cracking.
words.definition_en: Periodic examination of the concrete foundation for signs of settlement, cracking, grout degradation, and anchor bolt condition.
words.example_real: I was on site yesterday — the foundation inspection looks fine, but I'd like us to trend the data for a few more weeks.

words.example: Always wear a safety harness when working at height.
words.definition_en: Personal protective equipment comprising straps and attachment points worn by technicians to prevent falls during tower climbing.
words.example_real: The safety harness data over the past quarter shows a clear pattern — worth discussing with the OEM.

words.example: Lockout-tagout ensures isolation of hazardous energy.
words.definition_en: A safety procedure ensuring that dangerous equipment is properly shut off and cannot be restarted until maintenance work is completed.
words.example_real: The lockout tagout failed on turbine #12 during the grid dip — we're mobilizing the spare and a technician.

words.example: PPE is mandatory for all wind turbine maintenance work.
words.definition_en: Equipment worn by workers to minimize exposure to workplace hazards including helmets, gloves, eye protection, and fall arrest systems.
words.example_real: Can you present the personal protective equipment status at the steering committee? The lenders want a detailed update.

words.example: Complete a job safety analysis before starting any task.
words.definition_en: A systematic process of identifying hazards and implementing controls before commencing a specific maintenance task.
words.example_real: The safety analysis reading on turbine #7 was out of spec, so we're sending a crew up first thing tomorrow.

words.example: A rescue plan must be in place before tower climbing.
words.definition_en: A pre-established procedure detailing how an injured or incapacitated technician will be safely evacuated from the tower or hub.
words.example_real: Can you double-check the rescue plan status on the north string? SCADA flagged an intermittent fault overnight.

words.example: Every maintenance visit must conclude with a service report.
words.definition_en: A formal document recording all maintenance activities performed, findings, parts consumed, and any follow-up actions required.
words.example_real: We lost two days on service report because the significant wave height exceeded the vessel's working limit.

words.example: The maintenance log tracks all turbine service history.
words.definition_en: A chronological record of all maintenance, repair, and inspection activities performed on each turbine in the fleet.
words.example_real: We've extended the maintenance log inspection interval by six months based on good condition history.

words.example: The retrofit improved turbine efficiency by five percent.
words.definition_en: The modification of existing equipment with upgraded components or systems to improve performance or extend service life.
words.example_real: After that windstorm, we did a walkdown and found three retrofit issues. Nothing critical, but worth scheduling.

words.example: The OEM released an upgrade kit for the cooling system.
words.definition_en: A package of modified or improved components supplied by the manufacturer for retrofitting existing turbines.
words.example_real: We'll need to swap out the upgrade kit during the next planned outage — let's get the spare ordered now.

words.example: Life extension programs can add ten years of operation.
words.definition_en: A program of assessment, analysis, and targeted upgrades to extend wind turbine operational life beyond the original design lifetime.
words.example_real: The life extension work package is at 60% — tracking slightly behind, but recovery plan is in place.

words.example: Decommissioning involves safe removal and site restoration.
words.definition_en: The process of permanently taking a wind turbine or wind farm out of service and restoring the site to agreed conditions.
words.example_real: The decommissioning PM is due next Tuesday. I've blocked the turbine from 6 AM to 2 PM.

words.example: Repowering replaces old turbines with larger, more efficient ones.
words.definition_en: The process of replacing older wind turbines with newer, higher-capacity models, often using existing grid connections and site infrastructure.
words.example_real: Site team reported unusual noise from the repowering — I want engineering to do a root cause analysis.

words.example: Dismantling the old turbines required a 750-ton crane.
words.definition_en: The systematic removal and disassembly of wind turbine components during decommissioning or repowering activities.
words.example_real: We just finished the dismantling design review. Some good ideas came up for cutting cost without sacrificing quality.

words.example: Site restoration returns the land to its original state.
words.definition_en: The process of returning a decommissioned wind farm site to the condition specified in the planning consent and lease agreement.
words.example_real: The site restoration delivery was two units short. Can you expedite the remaining items?

words.example: Wind resource assessment determines the energy potential of a site.
words.definition_en: The process of measuring and modeling wind conditions at a prospective site to estimate long-term energy production.
words.example_real: I need your numbers on wind resource assessment before Friday. I'm presenting to the client's technical team.

words.example: A met mast measures wind speed at multiple heights.
words.definition_en: A meteorological tower equipped with anemometers, wind vanes, and other sensors at various heights to characterize the wind resource.
words.example_real: In the weekly coordination call, we agreed met mast is the priority — if it slips, COD moves.

words.example: LiDAR provides remote wind speed measurements up to 200 meters.
words.definition_en: A remote sensing technology using pulsed laser light to measure wind speed and direction at multiple heights without a physical tower.
words.example_real: The LiDAR measurement geotech results came in — we'll need to adjust the foundation design in that area.

words.example: SODAR uses sound waves to profile wind speeds.
words.definition_en: A remote sensing device that emits acoustic pulses and measures the Doppler shift of backscattered signals to determine wind profiles.
words.example_real: The EPC contractor raised a flag on SODAR measurement — I've put it on the risk register with a mitigation plan.

words.example: The measurement campaign must span at least 12 months.
words.definition_en: A defined period of systematic wind data collection at a prospective wind farm site using met masts and remote sensing devices.
words.example_real: RFQ responses for measurement campaign are in. Three suppliers shortlisted — site visits planned for next month.

words.example: The wind atlas maps resource potential across the region.
words.definition_en: A comprehensive mapping of wind speed and power density across a geographic area, used for regional planning and site screening.
words.example_real: We've engaged a consultant for the wind atlas analysis — it's required for the EIA submission.

words.example: Site screening identifies viable locations for wind farms.
words.definition_en: The initial evaluation process that filters potential wind farm sites based on resource, accessibility, grid proximity, and environmental constraints.
words.example_real: Your site screening proposal looks solid. A couple of points on the warranty — let's discuss.

words.example: The feasibility study assessed technical and economic viability.
words.definition_en: A comprehensive analysis evaluating the technical, economic, environmental, and regulatory aspects of a proposed wind farm project.
words.example_real: Can you present the feasibility study status at the steering committee? The lenders want a detailed update.

words.example: Energy yield assessment predicts annual production.
words.definition_en: A detailed calculation of expected annual energy production considering wind resource, turbine characteristics, and loss factors.
words.example_real: The EPC contractor raised a flag on energy yield assessment — I've put it on the risk register with a mitigation plan.

words.example: Wake modeling optimizes turbine layout to minimize losses.
words.definition_en: Computational simulation of wind speed reduction and turbulence increase behind turbines to quantify energy losses in wind farm arrays.
words.example_real: The wake modeling work package is at 60% — tracking slightly behind, but recovery plan is in place.

words.example: Micro-siting determines the exact position of each turbine.
words.definition_en: The process of positioning individual turbines within a wind farm area to maximize energy yield while respecting constraints.
words.example_real: The micro-siting geotech results came in — we'll need to adjust the foundation design in that area.

words.example: The wind farm layout must balance energy and constraints.
words.definition_en: The spatial arrangement of turbines, access roads, cabling, and infrastructure within a wind farm site boundary.
words.example_real: In the weekly coordination call, we agreed wind farm layout is the priority — if it slips, COD moves.

words.example: An EIA is required before construction approval.
words.definition_en: A formal study evaluating the potential environmental effects of a proposed wind farm, required for permitting in most jurisdictions.
words.example_real: Can you present the environmental impact assessment status at the steering committee? The lenders want a detailed update.

words.example: Avian studies assess risks to bird populations.
words.definition_en: A specialized ecological study evaluating the potential collision risk and habitat disturbance of wind turbines on bird species.
words.example_real: The avian study study results are promising — we're recommending moving to detailed design.

words.example: Noise assessment ensures compliance with local regulations.
words.definition_en: A study predicting and measuring the acoustic impact of wind turbines on nearby noise-sensitive receptors such as residences.
words.example_real: The noise assessment constraint mapping shows a few exclusion zones. We can work around them.

words.example: Shadow flicker analysis protects nearby residents.
words.definition_en: The alternating change in light intensity caused when rotating turbine blades pass between the sun and a receptor location.
words.example_real: Can you confirm the shadow flicker configuration matches the latest grid code revision?

words.example: The grid connection study confirmed adequate capacity.
words.definition_en: A technical study evaluating the feasibility, cost, and technical requirements for connecting a wind farm to the electricity network.
words.example_real: The grid connection study compliance test with the grid operator is scheduled for next Monday — fingers crossed.

words.example: The land lease agreement specifies rent and terms.
words.definition_en: A legal contract between the wind farm developer and landowner granting rights to install and operate turbines on the property.
words.example_real: The land lease agreement constraint mapping shows a few exclusion zones. We can work around them.

words.example: Planning consent was granted after a two-year process.
words.definition_en: Official permission from the local planning authority to construct and operate a wind farm at a specified location under defined conditions.
words.example_real: We need to talk about the planning consent lead time — it's longer than what's in the supply agreement.

words.example: The construction permit was issued after EIA approval.
words.definition_en: Regulatory authorization allowing the commencement of physical construction activities at the wind farm site.
words.example_real: I need your numbers on construction permit before Friday. I'm presenting to the client's technical team.

words.example: Each turbine has a pad-mounted transformer for voltage step-up.
words.definition_en: An outdoor transformer installed on a concrete pad at the base of each wind turbine, stepping up generator voltage to medium voltage collection level.
words.example_real: We need to verify the pad-mounted transformer protection settings before energization. No shortcuts on safety.

words.example: The collector substation aggregates power from all turbines.
words.definition_en: A substation that combines the output from multiple wind turbine feeder circuits before transmitting power to the grid interconnection point.
words.example_real: The collector substation harmonics study came back clean. We're good on the power quality side.

words.example: The main power transformer steps up voltage for transmission.
words.definition_en: The large transformer at the wind farm substation that increases the collection voltage to the transmission or grid interconnection voltage level.
words.example_real: The TSO approved the main power transformer relay settings after the second round of review.

words.example: GIS switchgear saves space in offshore substations.
words.definition_en: Compact high-voltage switchgear where the conductors and contacts are insulated by pressurized sulfur hexafluoride gas.
words.example_real: Can you confirm the gas-insulated switchgear configuration matches the latest grid code revision?

words.example: Circuit breakers protect equipment from electrical faults.
words.definition_en: An automatically operated electrical switch designed to protect an electrical circuit from damage caused by overcurrent or short circuit.
words.example_real: I was on site yesterday — the circuit breaker looks fine, but I'd like us to trend the data for a few more weeks.

words.example: Disconnect switches provide visible isolation for maintenance.
words.definition_en: A mechanical switching device that provides visible isolation of a circuit for safety during maintenance, but does not interrupt load current.
words.example_real: Make sure the crew reviews the disconnect switch JSA before starting work — it's a high-risk activity.

words.example: Reactive power compensation maintains voltage stability.
words.definition_en: Equipment and control strategies to inject or absorb reactive power, maintaining voltage within grid code requirements at the point of interconnection.
words.example_real: The reactive power compensation compliance test with the grid operator is scheduled for next Monday — fingers crossed.

words.example: The SVG provides fast reactive power support.
words.definition_en: A power electronics-based device that dynamically injects or absorbs reactive power to regulate voltage at the wind farm connection point.
words.example_real: We spotted an issue with the VAR during this morning's walkdown — logged and assigned to the day shift.

words.example: STATCOM enhances grid stability for large wind farms.
words.definition_en: A static synchronous compensator using voltage-source converter technology for dynamic reactive power control and voltage regulation.
words.example_real: The STATCOM failed on turbine #12 during the grid dip — we're mobilizing the spare and a technician.

words.example: Voltage regulators maintain output within grid requirements.
words.definition_en: A device or control system that automatically maintains the output voltage of the wind farm within a specified range.
words.example_real: The TSO approved the voltage regulator relay settings after the second round of review.

words.example: LVRT capability is essential for grid code compliance.
words.definition_en: The ability of a wind turbine to remain connected and support the grid during a temporary voltage dip, rather than disconnecting.
words.example_real: The low voltage ride through harmonics study came back clean. We're good on the power quality side.

words.example: HVRT protects turbines during grid overvoltage events.
words.definition_en: The capability of wind turbines to continue operating through temporary overvoltage conditions on the grid without tripping.
words.example_real: We need to verify the high voltage ride through protection settings before energization. No shortcuts on safety.

words.example: Modern wind farms provide frequency response to the grid.
words.definition_en: The automatic adjustment of wind turbine active power output in response to changes in grid frequency to help maintain system stability.
words.example_real: The frequency response harmonics study came back clean. We're good on the power quality side.

words.example: The grid code requires a power factor between 0.95 leading and lagging.
words.definition_en: The ratio of real power to apparent power in an AC electrical system, indicating how efficiently electrical power is being used.
words.example_real: We need to verify the power factor protection settings before energization. No shortcuts on safety.

words.example: Harmonic distortion must be below specified limits.
words.definition_en: Voltage or current waveform distortion caused by the presence of frequency components that are multiples of the fundamental power system frequency.
words.example_real: Let's schedule the harmonic distortion FAT for early August. We'll send our QA engineer.

words.example: Power quality monitoring ensures grid compliance.
words.definition_en: The characteristics of the electrical power supply including voltage stability, frequency, harmonics, flicker, and interruptions as delivered to the grid.
words.example_real: The power quality commissioning went really smoothly — your site engineer was excellent.

words.example: The SCADA system monitors and controls the entire wind farm.
words.definition_en: A supervisory control and data acquisition system that provides centralized monitoring, control, and data collection for all turbines.
words.example_real: The TSO approved the SCADA system relay settings after the second round of review.

words.example: The EMS optimizes wind farm dispatch to the grid.
words.definition_en: A software platform that optimizes the economic dispatch of the wind farm considering market prices, grid constraints, and asset conditions.
words.example_real: We just finished the energy management system design review. Some good ideas came up for cutting cost without sacrificing quality.

words.example: Fiber optic cables connect all turbines to the control center.
words.definition_en: A communication backbone using optical fibers to transmit high-speed data between wind turbines and the central SCADA system.
words.example_real: The fiber optic network compliance test with the grid operator is scheduled for next Monday — fingers crossed.

words.example: RTUs collect data from meteorological stations.
words.definition_en: A microprocessor-controlled device that interfaces sensors and actuators to the SCADA system, used for substation and met mast monitoring.
words.example_real: After that windstorm, we did a walkdown and found three remote terminal unit issues. Nothing critical, but worth scheduling.

words.example: The offshore wind farm is located 30 kilometers from shore.
words.definition_en: A wind farm installed in bodies of water, typically on the continental shelf, to harvest wind energy with generally higher and more consistent wind speeds.
words.example_real: The offshore wind farm monopile driving is complete. Transition piece grouting starts tomorrow.

words.example: The transition piece connects the monopile to the tower.
words.definition_en: A steel structure that bridges the foundation (monopile or jacket) to the turbine tower, providing a working platform and cable entry.
words.example_real: The transition piece installation is heavily weather-dependent — our marine coordinator is watching the forecast closely.

words.example: Monopile foundations are the most common offshore foundation type.
words.definition_en: A single large-diameter steel pipe driven into the seabed, forming the simplest and most common offshore wind turbine foundation.
words.example_real: We lost two days on monopile foundation because the significant wave height exceeded the vessel's working limit.

words.example: Jacket foundations are used in deeper waters.
words.definition_en: A space-frame steel structure with three or four legs anchored to the seabed with piles, suitable for water depths beyond 30 meters.
words.example_real: The jacket foundation monopile driving is complete. Transition piece grouting starts tomorrow.

words.example: Suction bucket foundations reduce installation noise.
words.definition_en: A bucket-shaped steel foundation installed by creating negative pressure inside to pull the structure into the seabed, avoiding pile driving noise.
words.example_real: Can you send over the updated suction bucket datasheet? Our engineering team has a few technical queries.

words.example: Floating foundations enable wind energy in deep waters.
words.definition_en: A buoyant substructure moored to the seabed that supports a wind turbine, enabling deployment in water depths exceeding 60 meters.
words.example_real: We're seeing some unusual readings on the floating foundation — let's pull the trend data for the last 30 days.

words.example: Semi-submersible platforms provide stability in deep water.
words.definition_en: A floating wind foundation consisting of connected columns and pontoons that derive stability from water plane area and ballasting.
words.example_real: Site team reported unusual noise from the semi-submersible platform — I want engineering to do a root cause analysis.

words.example: Mooring lines secure the floating platform to anchors.
words.definition_en: Chains, wire ropes, or synthetic ropes that connect a floating wind structure to anchors on the seabed.
words.example_real: The mooring line doesn't fully meet the spec in appendix C — can we set up a call to go through the deviation?

words.example: Drag anchors embed into the seabed when pulled.
words.definition_en: A type of anchor that penetrates and embeds into the seabed soil through horizontal dragging, developing holding capacity through soil resistance.
words.example_real: I was on site yesterday — the drag anchor looks fine, but I'd like us to trend the data for a few more weeks.

words.example: Submarine cables connect turbines to the offshore substation.
words.definition_en: A specially designed and armored power or fiber optic cable laid on or buried under the seabed for offshore wind applications.
words.example_real: We're using a jack-up for the submarine cable campaign to minimize weather downtime.

words.example: Cable burial protects against anchors and fishing gear.
words.definition_en: The process of burying submarine cables below the seabed to protect them from external damage and thermal derating.
words.example_real: The cable burial coating spec needs to meet C5-M for the offshore environment. Don't cut corners on this.

words.example: The offshore substation collects and steps up voltage.
words.definition_en: A platform or structure at sea that collects power from turbines and transforms it to higher voltage for efficient transmission to shore.
words.example_real: The offshore substation coating spec needs to meet C5-M for the offshore environment. Don't cut corners on this.

words.example: The export cable carries power from offshore to onshore.
words.definition_en: The high-voltage submarine cable that transmits the aggregated wind farm power from the offshore substation to the onshore grid connection point.
words.example_real: We lost two days on export cable because the significant wave height exceeded the vessel's working limit.

words.example: Array cables connect individual turbines within the farm.
words.definition_en: Medium-voltage submarine cables connecting wind turbines together in strings that feed into the offshore substation.
words.example_real: Can you double-check the array cable status on the north string? SCADA flagged an intermittent fault overnight.

words.example: Cable protection systems prevent damage at entry points.
words.definition_en: Articulated shells, bend restrictors, or rock dumping installed at the point where cables enter the foundation to prevent over-bending and abrasion.
words.example_real: Can you confirm the cable protection system configuration matches the latest grid code revision?

words.example: Scour protection prevents erosion around foundations.
words.definition_en: Rock, concrete mattresses, or geotextile materials placed around offshore foundations to prevent the seabed from being eroded by currents and waves.
words.example_real: The scour protection installation is heavily weather-dependent — our marine coordinator is watching the forecast closely.

words.example: Cathodic protection prevents steel corrosion underwater.
words.definition_en: An electrochemical technique using sacrificial anodes or impressed current to prevent corrosion of steel structures in seawater.
words.example_real: We're using a jack-up for the cathodic protection campaign to minimize weather downtime.

words.example: Marine growth increases structural loading on foundations.
words.definition_en: The accumulation of marine organisms such as barnacles, mussels, and algae on submerged structures, increasing diameter and hydrodynamic loading.
words.example_real: The marine growth monopile driving is complete. Transition piece grouting starts tomorrow.

words.example: Jack-up vessels provide a stable platform for offshore installation.
words.definition_en: A specialized vessel with extendable legs that can be lowered to the seabed, lifting the hull above the water to create a stable working platform.
words.example_real: The jack-up vessel monopile driving is complete. Transition piece grouting starts tomorrow.

words.example: Heavy lift vessels install large offshore structures.
words.definition_en: A specialized ship equipped with one or more high-capacity cranes capable of lifting and installing large offshore wind components.
words.example_real: We lost two days on heavy lift vessel because the significant wave height exceeded the vessel's working limit.

words.example: LFP batteries dominate stationary storage due to safety and cycle life.
words.definition_en: Denotes 磷酸铁锂; an important piece of terminology for practitioners.
words.example_real: When clients ask about lithium iron phosphate, I usually walk them through our test data — it speaks for itself.

words.example: NMC batteries offer higher energy density than LFP.
words.definition_en: Denotes 镍钴锰三元; an important piece of terminology for practitioners.
words.example_real: We're seeing a lot of C&I demand for nickel manganese cobalt, especially from manufacturers with high demand charges.

words.example: NCA batteries are primarily used in electric vehicles.
words.definition_en: Denotes 镍钴铝三元; an important piece of terminology for practitioners.
words.example_real: The client's main question was about nickel cobalt aluminum — they want to understand the revenue stacking.

words.example: Lithium-ion batteries dominate the energy storage market.
words.definition_en: A rechargeable battery type where lithium ions move between anode and cathode through an electrolyte during charge and discharge.
words.example_real: The customer's technical team dug deep on lithium ion battery during the site visit. We handled it well.

words.example: LFP batteries offer excellent safety and cycle life.
words.definition_en: A lithium iron phosphate cathode chemistry known for thermal stability, long cycle life, and lower cost but lower energy density than NMC.
words.example_real: I've put together a one-pager on LFP battery for the client. Happy to walk you through it.

words.example: Sodium-ion batteries offer a low-cost alternative to lithium.
words.definition_en: An emerging battery technology using abundant sodium instead of lithium, promising lower cost but currently lower energy density.
words.example_real: I've put together a one-pager on sodium ion battery for the client. Happy to walk you through it.

words.example: Flow batteries are ideal for long-duration storage.
words.definition_en: A rechargeable battery where energy is stored in liquid electrolytes contained in external tanks, allowing independent scaling of power and energy capacity.
words.example_real: We tested the flow battery under extreme conditions last month — the results were solid.

words.example: Solid-state batteries promise higher safety and energy density.
words.definition_en: Next-generation batteries using solid electrolytes instead of liquid, offering improved safety and potential for higher energy density.
words.example_real: The customer's technical team dug deep on solid state battery during the site visit. We handled it well.

words.example: LTO batteries excel in high-power, fast-charging applications.
words.definition_en: A lithium battery with a titanate anode offering ultra-fast charging, excellent low-temperature performance, and very long cycle life.
words.example_real: When clients ask about lithium titanate battery, I usually walk them through our test data — it speaks for itself.

words.example: Prismatic cells maximize packing density in battery modules.
words.definition_en: A rectangular-format battery cell enclosed in a rigid metal or plastic case, commonly used in energy storage and EV applications.
words.example_real: The key advantage of our prismatic cell approach is lower total cost of ownership over 15 years.

words.example: Cylindrical cells offer superior mechanical stability.
words.definition_en: A battery cell in a cylindrical metal can format, offering excellent mechanical robustness and automated manufacturing maturity.
words.example_real: We're seeing a strong trend toward cylindrical cell in the latest RFPs. It's becoming table stakes.

words.example: Pouch cells offer lightweight and flexible form factors.
words.definition_en: A battery cell packaged in a flexible aluminum-plastic laminate pouch, offering high energy density per kilogram but requiring external mechanical support.
words.example_real: In terms of pouch cell, our system consistently beats the spec. I can share the third-party test report.

words.example: Cell balancing ensures uniform voltage across the pack.
words.definition_en: The process of equalizing the state of charge of individual cells in a series-connected battery string to maximize usable capacity and prevent over-charge or over-discharge.
words.example_real: When clients ask about cell balancing, I usually walk them through our test data — it speaks for itself.

words.example: Thermal runaway is the most critical battery safety concern.
words.definition_en: A self-accelerating exothermic reaction inside a battery cell that can lead to fire or explosion if not contained.
words.example_real: We ran a detailed analysis on the thermal runaway and the results look promising — I'll share the report.

words.example: Battery degradation affects long-term project economics.
words.definition_en: The gradual loss of battery capacity and increase in internal resistance over time due to calendar aging and cycle aging mechanisms.
words.example_real: In terms of battery degradation, our system consistently beats the spec. I can share the third-party test report.

words.example: Capacity augmentation maintains rated energy over project life.
words.definition_en: The strategy of adding new battery modules over time to compensate for degradation and maintain the contracted energy capacity.
words.example_real: I've put together a one-pager on capacity augmentation for the client. Happy to walk you through it.

words.example: DC block refers to the battery's rated energy capacity.
words.definition_en: The total installed energy capacity on the DC side of the PCS, measured in megawatt-hours, representing the battery's nameplate storage capability.
words.example_real: The client's main question was about DC block — they want to understand the revenue stacking.

words.example: DC coupling reduces conversion losses in solar-plus-storage.
words.definition_en: An architecture where the battery is connected on the DC side of a shared inverter with PV, saving one power conversion stage.
words.example_real: The key advantage of our DC coupled storage approach is lower total cost of ownership over 15 years.

words.example: AC coupling offers flexibility for retrofitting existing sites.
words.definition_en: An architecture where the battery has its own dedicated inverter and connects on the AC side, suitable for standalone or retrofit applications.
words.example_real: We're seeing a lot of C&I demand for AC coupled storage, especially from manufacturers with high demand charges.

words.example: The PCS converts between DC battery and AC grid power.
words.definition_en: A bidirectional inverter that converts DC power from batteries to AC for grid injection and rectifies AC to DC for battery charging.
words.example_real: I ran the power conversion system numbers for three scenarios. Even the conservative case looks attractive.

words.example: Bidirectional inverters enable both charging and discharging.
words.definition_en: A power electronic converter capable of both inverting DC to AC and rectifying AC to DC, essential for battery energy storage charging and discharging.
words.example_real: The customer's technical team dug deep on bidirectional inverter during the site visit. We handled it well.

words.example: Grid-forming inverters can create a stable grid independently.
words.definition_en: An inverter that can establish and regulate voltage and frequency independently, enabling islanded operation and black start capability.
words.example_real: The grid-forming inverter test results go into the commissioning report for the utility's review.

words.example: The BMS monitors and protects individual battery cells.
words.definition_en: An electronic system that monitors cell voltages, temperatures, and currents, performs cell balancing, and protects the battery from unsafe operating conditions.
words.example_real: We're seeing a strong trend toward battery management system in the latest RFPs. It's becoming table stakes.

words.example: SOC indicates the remaining battery capacity as a percentage.
words.definition_en: A measurement representing the current charge level of a battery as a percentage of its rated capacity, typically estimated through coulomb counting and voltage correlation.
words.example_real: We're seeing a lot of C&I demand for state of charge, especially from manufacturers with high demand charges.

words.example: SOH measures battery degradation over time.
words.definition_en: A metric comparing the current battery performance to its original specification, typically expressed as the ratio of present capacity to rated capacity.
words.example_real: The state of health payback for this site is under four years — the CFO was genuinely excited.

words.example: Deeper discharge reduces battery cycle life.
words.definition_en: The percentage of battery capacity that has been discharged, with deeper DoD generally resulting in faster degradation and fewer total cycles.
words.example_real: The depth of discharge capex is running above budget — let's look at optimization before the next review.

words.example: Round trip efficiency affects project revenue calculations.
words.definition_en: The ratio of energy discharged from a storage system to the energy required to charge it, expressed as a percentage, accounting for all conversion and auxiliary losses.
words.example_real: The round trip efficiency commissioning checklist is complete. All tests passed. Ready for the capacity test.

words.example: The EMS optimizes battery dispatch for maximum revenue.
words.definition_en: A software platform that controls when and how the battery charges and discharges based on market signals, grid conditions, and asset constraints.
words.example_real: For this project, we're recommending a energy management system configuration to meet the 4-hour duration target.

words.example: Battery containers house the storage system in a standard footprint.
words.definition_en: A prefabricated 20-foot or 40-foot ISO container integrating battery racks, BMS, HVAC, fire suppression, and auxiliary systems as a turnkey unit.
words.example_real: We tested the battery container under extreme conditions last month — the results were solid.

words.example: Battery racks organize cells into manageable units.
words.definition_en: A structural frame holding multiple battery modules in series and parallel configuration, forming a building block of the overall storage system.
words.example_real: In terms of battery rack, our system consistently beats the spec. I can share the third-party test report.

words.example: Battery modules combine cells into replaceable units.
words.definition_en: An intermediate assembly of series-connected cells with integrated sensing, structural support, and thermal management interfaces.
words.example_real: We do a thorough battery module inspection every month as part of the preventative maintenance schedule.

words.example: The HVAC system maintains optimal battery temperature.
words.definition_en: Heating, ventilation, and air conditioning system that controls the internal environment of the battery container for optimal performance and safety.
words.example_real: I updated the HVAC system section in the technical proposal. Can you give it a quick review?

words.example: The BESS project was commissioned ahead of schedule.
words.definition_en: Abbreviation for Battery Energy Storage System, a facility using batteries to store electrical energy for later use.
words.example_real: During commissioning, the BESS parameters needed a fine-tune. Rectified on the spot.

words.example: Four-hour storage duration is most common for utility-scale.
words.definition_en: The length of time a storage system can discharge at its rated power, determined by the ratio of energy capacity to power rating.
words.example_real: The storage duration capex is running above budget — let's look at optimization before the next review.

words.example: LCOS compares the lifecycle cost of different storage technologies.
words.definition_en: A metric representing the total lifecycle cost per unit of discharged energy, analogous to LCOE for generation assets.
words.example_real: I ran the levelized cost of storage numbers for three scenarios. Even the conservative case looks attractive.

words.example: Revenue stacking is essential for storage project viability.
words.definition_en: The practice of earning income from multiple market products and services simultaneously using the same storage asset.
words.example_real: The client's main question was about revenue stacking — they want to understand the revenue stacking.

words.example: Energy arbitrage buys low and sells high in electricity markets.
words.definition_en: The practice of purchasing electricity during low-price periods to charge the battery and selling it during high-price periods to discharge.
words.example_real: I ran the energy arbitrage numbers for three scenarios. Even the conservative case looks attractive.

words.example: Batteries provide superior frequency regulation response.
words.definition_en: A grid service where storage assets rapidly adjust their output to maintain the power system frequency at its nominal value.
words.example_real: The frequency regulation system activated during the drill — response time was well within the target.

words.example: Capacity market payments provide a stable revenue base.
words.definition_en: A market mechanism where generators and storage resources are paid for committing to be available during periods of system stress.
words.example_real: The capacity market payback for this site is under four years — the CFO was genuinely excited.

words.example: Ancillary services support grid reliability and power quality.
words.definition_en: Support services required to maintain grid reliability including frequency regulation, voltage support, spinning reserve, and black start capability.
words.example_real: We're seeing a lot of C&I demand for ancillary services, especially from manufacturers with high demand charges.

words.example: The project IRR must exceed the investment hurdle rate.
words.definition_en: Internal Rate of Return, the discount rate that makes the net present value of all project cash flows equal to zero, used as a key investment metric.
words.example_real: The client's main question was about IRR — they want to understand the revenue stacking.

words.example: Capacity lease provides predictable storage revenue.
words.definition_en: A contractual arrangement where storage capacity is leased to a utility or large customer for a fixed monthly fee rather than participating in merchant markets.
words.example_real: The client's main question was about capacity lease — they want to understand the revenue stacking.

words.example: Merchant storage projects carry higher revenue risk.
words.definition_en: Storage projects that sell all output and services into wholesale markets without long-term contracts, exposed to market price volatility.
words.example_real: For this project, we're recommending a merchant storage configuration to meet the 4-hour duration target.

words.example: A tolling agreement provides fixed payments for storage services.
words.definition_en: A long-term contract where a storage owner receives fixed payments for making the asset available, while the offtaker controls dispatch and keeps all market revenue.
words.example_real: We're seeing a lot of C&I demand for tolling agreement, especially from manufacturers with high demand charges.

words.example: The battery supply agreement includes performance warranties.
words.definition_en: A contract between the project developer and battery manufacturer covering pricing, delivery schedule, and performance guarantees for the battery system.
words.example_real: I've put together a one-pager on battery supply agreement for the client. Happy to walk you through it.

words.example: OEM warranty covers battery performance over the contract term.
words.definition_en: A manufacturer's guarantee covering defects in materials and workmanship, and in the case of batteries, typically including a performance degradation curve.
words.example_real: During commissioning, the OEM warranty parameters needed a fine-tune. Rectified on the spot.

words.example: C&I storage reduces peak demand charges.
words.definition_en: Behind-the-meter battery systems installed at commercial or industrial facilities primarily to reduce peak demand charges and provide backup power.
words.example_real: I ran the commercial and industrial storage numbers for three scenarios. Even the conservative case looks attractive.

words.example: Peak shaving reduces electricity costs for businesses.
words.definition_en: Using stored energy to reduce the maximum power drawn from the grid during peak periods, thereby lowering demand charges.
words.example_real: The peak shaving payback for this site is under four years — the CFO was genuinely excited.

words.example: Demand charge management is the primary C&I storage use case.
words.definition_en: The strategic use of energy storage to limit the peak power drawn from the grid, directly reducing the demand-based portion of commercial electricity bills.
words.example_real: The client's main question was about demand charge management — they want to understand the revenue stacking.

words.example: TOU optimization shifts consumption to off-peak hours.
words.definition_en: Charging batteries during low-rate periods and discharging during high-rate periods to minimize total electricity costs under time-varying tariffs.
words.example_real: The time of use optimization payback for this site is under four years — the CFO was genuinely excited.

words.example: Behind-the-meter storage serves on-site load directly.
words.definition_en: Energy storage installed on the customer side of the utility meter, primarily serving the customer's own load rather than exporting to the grid.
words.example_real: We're seeing a strong trend toward behind the meter in the latest RFPs. It's becoming table stakes.

words.example: Front-of-meter storage participates directly in wholesale markets.
words.definition_en: Storage connected on the utility side of the meter, participating in wholesale electricity markets and providing grid services at the transmission or distribution level.
words.example_real: I ran the front of meter numbers for three scenarios. Even the conservative case looks attractive.

words.example: Microgrids can operate independently from the main grid.
words.definition_en: A localized group of electricity sources and loads that can operate connected to or independently from the main grid, typically incorporating storage for stability.
words.example_real: The grid operator wants more detail on microgrid before they'll sign off on the interconnection.

words.example: Islanding capability is critical for backup power applications.
words.definition_en: The ability of a distributed energy resource or microgrid to continue powering local loads when disconnected from the main utility grid.
words.example_real: We're coordinating with the TSO on islanding — should have an answer by early next week.

words.example: Black start capability restores power after a complete outage.
words.definition_en: The ability of a power source to start up and energize part of the grid without relying on external power from the transmission network.
words.example_real: The grid operator wants more detail on black start before they'll sign off on the interconnection.

words.example: Synthetic inertia provides fast frequency response from batteries.
words.definition_en: A control function that enables power electronic-interfaced resources to emulate the inertial response traditionally provided by rotating machines.
words.example_real: We need to demonstrate synthetic inertia capability to meet the frequency response requirements.

words.example: Storage can defer transmission and distribution upgrades.
words.definition_en: The use of energy storage to absorb excess generation or supply local load to avoid overloading constrained grid infrastructure.
words.example_real: The grid congestion relief test results go into the commissioning report for the utility's review.

words.example: Solar plus storage provides firm, dispatchable renewable power.
words.definition_en: A hybrid project combining solar PV generation with battery storage to provide more predictable, dispatchable output and capture higher-value evening prices.
words.example_real: The key advantage of our solar plus storage approach is lower total cost of ownership over 15 years.

words.example: Wind plus storage smooths intermittent generation.
words.definition_en: A hybrid project pairing wind generation with battery storage to reduce variability and capture higher prices during low-wind periods.
words.example_real: We presented the wind plus storage design to the developer yesterday. They're keen to move forward.

words.example: DERs include rooftop solar, storage, and EV chargers.
words.definition_en: Small-scale power generation or storage technologies located close to the point of consumption, including rooftop solar, batteries, and electric vehicles.
words.example_real: I updated the distributed energy resource section in the technical proposal. Can you give it a quick review?

words.example: A VPP aggregates distributed storage into dispatchable capacity.
words.definition_en: A cloud-based platform that aggregates and optimizes the dispatch of numerous distributed energy resources as if they were a single power plant.
words.example_real: The virtual power plant commissioning checklist is complete. All tests passed. Ready for the capacity test.

words.example: Demand response programs pay customers to reduce load.
words.definition_en: A program where electricity consumers voluntarily reduce their consumption during peak periods in exchange for financial incentives from the grid operator.
words.example_real: The customer's technical team dug deep on demand response during the site visit. We handled it well.
